sourceMovie.title: Little Nicky
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Little Nicky (2000) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Steven Brill's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Beetlejuice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Little Nicky clicked for you, Beetlejuice (1988) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Little Nicky?
faq.answer: Try Beetlejuice, Enchanted, Good Fortune—each shares DNA with Little Nicky while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Little Nicky?
faq.answer: Little Nicky (2000) is best described as Comedy, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Little Nicky worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
